message:
feat(lipgloss): library improvements and dependency updates

Updates to the lipgloss styling library:
- Cargo.toml dependency updates
- Library API enhancements in lib.rs
- Beads issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -25,7 +25,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1g80","title":"E2E scenario: file picker navigate + preview","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that exercises the Files page (bubbles FilePicker + preview).

Scenario outline:
1) Launch demo_showcase in deterministic headless mode.
2) Navigate to Files.
3) Use the file picker to:
   - move selection
   - enter a directory (if supported)
   - select a file
4) Validate preview behavior:
   - preview pane becomes non-empty
   - breadcrumb / filename reflects the selected entry
   - selecting a markdown fixture renders via glamour (not raw markdown), using the current theme

Notes:
- Avoid relying on the host filesystem. Use the demo’s deterministic fixture directory (from the asset strategy / Config).

Acceptance:
- Deterministic in CI.
- Failure artifacts include the directory listing, selected filename, and the last rendered Files frame.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.191594688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564367389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564301366Z","close_reason":"Integrated Files page into app (Page enum, Pages struct, routing). Added E2E tests for file picker navigation and preview functionality. Tests verify: fixture mode, j/k navigation, Enter to select/enter, Backspace to go back, and hidden file toggle.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","files","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-1wtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.230688217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-22c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:58.608108725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.221867959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.217633407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.226320857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.213212187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gdn","title":"Add lipgloss individual border edge colors and Transform method","description":"Add per-edge border colors and Transform method to Lipgloss.

Scope:
- Extend border style API to set color per edge (top/right/bottom/left).
- Implement Transform method for style manipulation consistent with Go (e.g., normalize or mutate styles).
- Add unit tests for per-edge colors and Transform behavior.
- Update docs if API changes are user-visible.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Per-edge border colors render correctly in multiple combinations.
- Transform method behaves as documented and has unit coverage.
- Conformance fixtures updated if needed to include per-edge color scenarios.","notes":"Files: crates/lipgloss/src/style.rs, border.rs, renderer.rs. Maintain backward compatibility where practical (but no shims).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:48.923880193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.451539959Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.450257984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed modes/flags (finalize based on UX concept):
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--theme-file <path>` (optional)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (run without alt-screen; useful for debugging + demonstrating bubbletea println/printf)
- `--self-check` (headless CI-friendly render + exit)

Optional subcommands:
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (wish integration; feature-gated)
- `export --html <path>` / `export --plain <path>` (optional: export snapshots)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory.
- Flags map directly to `demo_showcase::Config` fields.
- Error messages are user-friendly (bad theme name, bad seed, missing file).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539935175Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539870655Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884573066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.830556320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1isw","title":"huh: Fix FilePicker single-file selection edge case","description":"# Bug: FilePicker Cannot Select Single File

## Problem Statement
In the FilePicker navigation logic, the down-arrow condition prevents
selecting the only file when there's exactly one file:

```rust
// Lines 3660-3663
if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1) {
    self.selected_index += 1;
    // ...
}
```

When files.len() == 1:
- saturating_sub(1) = 0
- condition: 0 < 0 = FALSE
- navigation does nothing

## Root Cause Analysis
The condition `< len - 1` is meant to prevent going past the last item,
but it also prevents selecting the first (and only) item when starting
from index 0.

Actually, the bug is in the logic design. The condition should allow
moving TO the last item, not just items BEFORE the last.

Wait, re-reading: if selected_index is 0 and len is 1:
- Can't move down because 0 < 0 is false
But the item at index 0 IS selectable - it's already selected by default.

The real issue might be different - let me trace the full logic.

## Actual Issue
The condition prevents moving DOWN when already at last item, which is correct.
But if the initial state has selected_index pointing elsewhere or if there's
a reset that doesn't account for single-item lists, selection could be broken.

Let me re-examine:
```rust
// When len=1, saturating_sub(1)=0
// selected_index starts at 0 (presumably)
// Down key: 0 < 0 is false, no change (correct - already at end)
// Up key: 0 > 0 is false, no change (correct - already at start)
```

This is actually correct for navigation. The bug might be elsewhere -
perhaps in initialization or the actual selection/confirm logic.

## Further Investigation Needed
1. Check initial selected_index value for single-file directories
2. Check if Enter/confirm works with single file
3. Check if the file is visually highlighted

## Potential Fix Areas
If init doesn't set selected_index correctly:
```rust
fn set_files(&mut self, files: Vec<PathBuf>) {
    self.files = files;
    self.selected_index = 0.min(self.files.len().saturating_sub(1));
}
```

If confirm doesn't work:
```rust
fn confirm(&self) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    self.files.get(self.selected_index).cloned()
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn filepicker_single_file() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/single-file-dir");
    // Simulate single file
    picker.files = vec![PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")];
    picker.selected_index = 0;
    
    // Should be able to select it
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("only_file.txt"));
    assert!(/* file is highlighted */);
    
    // Confirm should return the file
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert_eq!(selected, Some(PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")));
}

#[test]
fn filepicker_empty_directory() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/empty-dir");
    picker.files = vec![];
    
    // Should handle gracefully
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("No files") || view.is_empty() == false);
    
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert!(selected.is_none());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-file directories work correctly
- [ ] Empty directories handled gracefully
- [ ] Navigation works for 0, 1, and many files
- [ ] Selection/confirm works in all cases
- [ ] Tests added for edge cases","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.943176512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967235547Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","filepicker","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1isw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967192347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Problem Statement

selecting the only file when there's exactly one file:

Let me re-examine:
```rust
// When len=1, saturating_sub(1)=0
// selected_index starts at 0 (presumably)
// Down key: 0 < 0 is false, no change (correct - already at end)
```

```rust
fn set_files(&mut self, files: Vec<PathBuf>) {
```

```rust

```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn filepicker_single_file() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/single-file-dir");
    // Simulate single file
    picker.files = vec![PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")];
    picker.selected_index = 0;

    assert_eq!(selected, Some(PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")));
}

#[test]
fn filepicker_empty_directory() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/empty-dir");
    picker.files = vec![];
    
    // Should handle gracefully
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("No files") || view.is_empty() == false);
    
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert!(selected.is_none());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-file directories work correctly
- [ ] Empty directories handled gracefully
- [ ] Navigation works for 0, 1, and many files
- [ ] Selection/confirm works in all cases
- [ ] Tests added for edge cases","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.943176512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:21:00.669923787Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","filepicker","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1isw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967192347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1knu","title":"Quality: headless self-check mode (CI-friendly)","description":"Add a non-interactive mode (e.g., `demo_showcase --self-check`) that validates the demo can boot and render headlessly.

Why:
- CI needs a fast "does it basically work" signal without a real TTY.
- Self-check also prevents subtle regressions in bootstrap/theming/rendering.

What self-check does:
- Construct the Config (deterministic defaults).
- Bootstrap the App model.
- Render a small set of views/pages (at a fixed size), for example:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
- Optionally run a tiny scripted navigation (no user input required).

Output:
- On success: exit 0.
- On failure:
  - exit non-zero
  - print a concise error + where artifacts were written
  - write a small artifact bundle (last rendered frame, config snapshot) under `target/demo_showcase_e2e/` or similar.

Constraints:
- Must work in `TERM=dumb` / no-tty environments.
- Must never hang (max-step/time guard).

Acceptance:
- Self-check runs quickly and deterministically in CI.
- Failures are actionable without rerunning locally.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.630984785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:34.964049662Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:34.963982256Z","close_reason":"Implemented headless self-check mode using E2ERunner. The --self-check flag navigates through Dashboard, Jobs, Logs, and Docs pages, validates each renders correctly, and exits 0 on success. Works in CI environments without a TTY.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:35.814496203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:26.513764136Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.649380944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:29.218612410Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kx7","title":"E2E: extend CI script to run full scenario suite","description":"Extend the E2E runner script to run the full demo_showcase scenario suite (not just smoke), with great logging and CI-friendly controls.

Why:
- The demo_showcase is a huge integration surface; regressions will happen.
- A single, well-instrumented E2E entrypoint makes it easy to validate “everything still works” locally and in CI.

Requirements:
- Profiles:
  - `smoke` (fast): minimal navigation, no panics, basic rendering assertions.
  - `full` (slower): runs every scenario and validates key outputs/artifacts.
  - optional `nightly` (extra slow): stress/resizes, multiple seeds, async-enabled run.
- Scenario selection:
  - allow selecting a subset (e.g., `--scenario docs` or `--scenario wizard,logs`).
- Determinism:
  - accepts a seed (or chooses and prints one) and writes it into the artifact directory.
- Observability:
  - structured JSONL log output + per-step timing
  - on failure, print a clear summary including artifact path and last N steps
- Exit codes:
  - non-zero on failure; zero on success.

Acceptance:
- CI can choose which profile to run (PR vs nightly) without editing the script.
- Failures are actionable: the artifact directory contains enough info to reproduce.

Testing:
- The script itself is validated by running at least the smoke suite in CI.
- Full suite runs either on demand or on nightly schedule.

Notes:
- Keep this script the canonical entrypoint; avoid multiple half-overlapping scripts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.825203081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:28.537686113Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-1g80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:31.237245937Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3449","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:42.979497295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ecr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:29.940423979Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3nrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:41.602626554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3spt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:32.598620003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-6wkl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:28.537657400Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-miqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:44.326429546Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.846212242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m7x","title":"E2E scenario: ssh mode smoke (optional/manual)","description":"Optional E2E scenario for wish integration.

Constraints:
- This may be too heavy for default CI.

Plan:
- Generate/use a temp host key.
- Start `demo_showcase ssh` on localhost with an ephemeral port.
- Connect with an SSH client (or a minimal russh client) and ensure:
  - the server accepts a session
  - the UI renders at least one frame
  - a quit sequence cleanly closes the session

Logging:
- Capture server logs + client transcript to artifacts.

Acceptance:
- Provides high confidence that wish integration stays alive over time.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:31.040331959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:33.585303389Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","ssh","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-32ba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:33.585268063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-3xs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:32.328836611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:31.067983923Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bz7","title":"demo_showcase data: deterministic generator + seed support","description":"Implement a generator that produces believable initial data.

Requirements:
- Seedable randomness (take a `seed: u64` parameter from Config/CLI, but keep generator API independent of clap).
- Avoid external network dependencies.
- Provide enough variety for lists/tables/filters.

Acceptance:
- Two runs with the same seed produce the same initial dataset.
- Unit tests can call the generator directly with a fixed seed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:50.646747021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:52:32.907828002Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:52:32.907763852Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bz7","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:50.659331020Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3bz7","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:45.718479299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3chy","title":"demo_showcase: optional SSH server mode (wish)","description":"Add an optional mode that serves the showcase over SSH (via `wish`) to prove the remote/SSH story is viable.

Why:
- Wish is one of the Charm ecosystem’s most compelling differentiators.
- Serving the same UI over SSH demonstrates composability: bubbletea Program IO abstraction + wish sessions + terminal capability negotiation.

Requirements:
- CLI:
  - `demo_showcase --ssh <addr>` (e.g., `:2222` or `0.0.0.0:2222`) starts the server.
  - host key UX:
    - accept `--host-key <path>` and/or generate a key if missing (decide in child tasks)
- Session behavior:
  - each SSH connection gets its own app instance (or a curated subset if full UI is too heavy)
  - wire bubbletea input/output to the SSH session streams
  - handle PTY sizing and resize events (best-effort)
- Security/ergonomics:
  - basic auth/middleware polish (simple but not embarrassing)
  - clear logs + startup banner (address, auth mode, host key path)

Acceptance:
- Running the server and connecting with `ssh -p 2222 localhost` launches the UI and is usable.
- Works with minimal setup beyond a host key.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - argument parsing and config wiring for ssh mode
  - host-key path validation logic
- E2E scripts:
  - a CI-safe “handshake + banner” test (non-interactive) that proves the server starts and accepts connections.
  - a local “interactive smoke” script (optional) that drives basic navigation via SSH and captures logs/artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep this optional: the main value of demo_showcase should not depend on SSH being enabled.
- Be realistic about CI environments; gate the heavier SSH tests appropriately.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.593755071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:44:44.471178493Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ssh","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3chy","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.612205255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d1w","title":"Dashboard: mouse affordances (clickable cards)","description":"Add mouse affordances to the Dashboard so it feels like a modern interactive console.

Why:
- Mouse support is an "advanced" feature that many terminal apps skip.
- When done well (subtle highlights + click actions), it makes the demo feel shockingly polished.

Behaviors:
- Click to select/focus a dashboard card/widget.
- Click on obvious affordances (e.g., "View logs" / "Open job") to navigate.
- Hover/selection highlight (keyboard focus and mouse selection should be visually consistent).

Constraints:
- Must degrade gracefully when mouse is disabled (Config toggle) or terminal doesn’t support it.
- Wheel scroll should not break layout; only scroll the active viewport/list when applicable.

Acceptance:
- Click selects a card and visibly focuses it.
- Mouse interactions never steal focus unexpectedly or cause layout corruption.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.759013697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:54.253286979Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:54.253217098Z","close_reason":"Implemented dashboard mouse affordances: clickable cards with visual selection highlighting, keyboard navigation (j/k/Tab/arrows), Enter to navigate to Services/Jobs pages, mouse click detection with hit testing, and 4 new tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:25.679670153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.778296349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:24.421135419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3d69","title":"bubbletea: Add debug logging for channel disconnection","description":"# Task: Add Debug Logging for Channel Disconnection

## Problem Statement
Throughout the event loop, channel send errors are silently discarded:

\`\`\`rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Error ignored
\`\`\`

When the main thread exits (panic, quit, etc.), spawned command threads
continue executing but their results disappear into the void. There's no
logging or visibility into these "orphaned" command executions.

## Affected Locations
- Line 610: External message forwarding
- Line 628: Custom input parser
- Line 680-690: Event messages
- Line 838: Batch command results
- Line 847: Sequence command results
- Line 852: Normal command results

## Impact
- **Debugging difficulty**: Hard to diagnose when commands execute but effects don't appear
- **Resource waste**: Commands continue executing after they're no longer needed
- **Silent failures**: No indication that something went wrong

## Solution Design

### Use tracing for Debug Logging
\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{debug, warn, instrument};

// Replace:
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);

// With:
if let Err(e) = tx_clone.send(msg) {
    debug!(
        target: "bubbletea::command",
        error = %e,
        command_type = ?cmd_type,
        "Command result dropped - receiver disconnected"
    );
}
\`\`\`

### Structured Logging Fields
\`\`\`rust
struct CommandContext {
    command_id: u64,
    command_type: &'static str,
    batch_id: Option<u64>,
}

// Log with context
debug!(
    target: "bubbletea",
    command_id = ctx.command_id,
    command_type = ctx.command_type,
    batch_id = ?ctx.batch_id,
    "Command result dropped"
);
\`\`\`

### Log Levels
- **trace**: All channel sends (very verbose, for deep debugging)
- **debug**: Failed sends during normal operation
- **warn**: Failed sends that indicate unexpected state

## Implementation Steps
1. Add tracing feature flag (already present)
2. Create helper function for logged send
3. Replace all \`let _ = tx.send()\` with logged version
4. Add command context tracking where feasible
5. Add documentation example
6. Add integration test for log capture

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
use tracing_test::traced_test;

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn channel_disconnection_is_logged() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    drop(rx);  // Disconnect receiver
    
    // This should log at debug level
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result dropped"));
    assert!(logs_contain("test_command"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn successful_send_logs_at_trace_level() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    // Trace level logs successful sends
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result sent"));
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Test
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn logs_include_command_context() {
    use tracing_subscriber::layer::SubscriberExt;
    
    let (captured, guard) = setup_log_capture();
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel);
    let cmd = Command::new(|| {
        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
        Some(Message::new("delayed"))
    });
    
    program.send(cmd);
    program.quit();  // Quit before command completes
    
    // Command result should be logged as dropped
    let logs = captured.into_inner();
    assert!(logs.iter().any(|l| l.contains("dropped")));
}
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: bubbletea_logging_e2e.sh

set -e
echo "=== bubbletea Channel Logging Tests ==="

# Enable trace logging
export RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug

# Run tests with log capture
cargo test --package bubbletea channel_disconnection -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log

# Verify expected log messages appear
if grep -q "Command result dropped" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Channel disconnection logging works"
else
    echo "[FAIL] Channel disconnection not logged"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify structured fields present
if grep -q "command_type" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Structured logging fields present"
else
    echo "[WARN] Missing structured fields - check log format"
fi

echo "=== Test Complete ==="
\`\`\`

+{"id":"bd-3d69","title":"bubbletea: Add debug logging for channel disconnection","description":"# Task: Add Debug Logging for Channel Disconnection

## Problem Statement
Throughout the event loop, channel send errors are silently discarded:

\`\`\`rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Error ignored
\`\`\`

When the main thread exits (panic, quit, etc.), spawned command threads
continue executing but their results disappear into the void. There's no
logging or visibility into these "orphaned" command executions.

## Affected Locations
- Line 610: External message forwarding
- Line 628: Custom input parser
- Line 680-690: Event messages
- Line 838: Batch command results
- Line 847: Sequence command results
- Line 852: Normal command results

## Impact
- **Debugging difficulty**: Hard to diagnose when commands execute but effects don't appear
- **Resource waste**: Commands continue executing after they're no longer needed
- **Silent failures**: No indication that something went wrong

## Solution Design

### Use tracing for Debug Logging
\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{debug, warn, instrument};

// Replace:
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);

// With:
if let Err(e) = tx_clone.send(msg) {
    debug!(
        target: "bubbletea::command",
        error = %e,
        command_type = ?cmd_type,
        "Command result dropped - receiver disconnected"
    );
}
\`\`\`

### Structured Logging Fields
\`\`\`rust
struct CommandContext {
    command_id: u64,
    command_type: &'static str,
    batch_id: Option<u64>,
}

// Log with context
debug!(
    target: "bubbletea",
    command_id = ctx.command_id,
    command_type = ctx.command_type,
    batch_id = ?ctx.batch_id,
    "Command result dropped"
);
\`\`\`

### Log Levels
- **trace**: All channel sends (very verbose, for deep debugging)
- **debug**: Failed sends during normal operation
- **warn**: Failed sends that indicate unexpected state

## Implementation Steps
1. Add tracing feature flag (already present)
2. Create helper function for logged send
3. Replace all \`let _ = tx.send()\` with logged version
4. Add command context tracking where feasible
5. Add documentation example
6. Add integration test for log capture

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust

fn channel_disconnection_is_logged() {

    drop(rx);  // Disconnect receiver
    
    // This should log at debug level
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result dropped"));
    assert!(logs_contain("test_command"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn successful_send_logs_at_trace_level() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    // Trace level logs successful sends
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result sent"));
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Test
\`\`\`rust

    use tracing_subscriber::layer::SubscriberExt;
    
    let (captured, guard) = setup_log_capture();

        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));

    program.quit();  // Quit before command completes
    
    // Command result should be logged as dropped
    let logs = captured.into_inner();
    assert!(logs.iter().any(|l| l.contains("dropped")));
}

 {"id":"bd-adw0","title":"Property Tests: harmonica spring physics","description":"Property-based tests for harmonica spring animation physics.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify spring physics invariants
and find edge cases through random input generation.

## Properties to Test

### Convergence
- [ ] For any (stiffness > 0, damping > 0), animation converges
- [ ] Convergence time bounded for given parameters
- [ ] Final value equals target within tolerance

### Stability
- [ ] No NaN or Inf values for any input
- [ ] Values don't explode (bounded growth)
- [ ] Consistent results for same input

### Physical Correctness
- [ ] More stiffness = faster response
- [ ] More damping = less oscillation
- [ ] Zero damping = sustained oscillation
- [ ] Critical damping = no overshoot

### Frame Independence
- [ ] Result similar regardless of frame rate
- [ ] Large dt doesn't break physics
- [ ] Small dt accumulated = large dt single

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn spring_always_converges(
        stiffness in 0.1..1000.0f64,
        damping in 0.1..100.0f64,
        target in -1000.0..1000.0f64,
    ) {
        let spring = Spring::new(stiffness, damping);
        // Simulate many frames
        // Assert converges to target
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- No crashes for any random input
- Physics invariants always hold
- Edge cases documented and handled","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:52.362702645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:56.297788477Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harmonica","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-adw0","depends_on_id":"bd-228s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:56.297757189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-adw0","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:52.397263463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-awkz","title":"lipgloss: Fix incomplete OSC sequence termination handling","description":"# Bug: Incomplete OSC Sequence Termination Handling

## Problem Statement
In lib.rs lines 403-411, the OSC sequence handling doesn't properly validate
the String Terminator (ST) format:

```rust
State::Osc => {
    if c == '\x07' {
        state = State::Normal;  // BEL terminator - correct
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        // Handle ST (ESC \) - we see ESC, transition to Esc to handle the backslash
        state = State::Esc;  // BUG: doesn't verify \ follows
    }
}
```

## Technical Background
OSC (Operating System Command) sequences have format:
```
ESC ] Ps ; Pt BEL      (7-bit, BEL terminator)
ESC ] Ps ; Pt ESC \    (7-bit, ST terminator)
```

The current code sees ESC and transitions to Esc state, but:
1. Doesn't verify the next character is \
2. If the next char is [, it would incorrectly start a new CSI sequence
3. Incomplete ST at end of string leaves state in Esc

## Example Malformed Input
```
\x1b]0;title\x1b[31m  (OSC followed immediately by CSI - no proper terminator)
```
Current behavior: After seeing \x1b in OSC, transitions to Esc, then [ transitions to Csi.
The "0;title" is ignored, and "[31m" is treated as a CSI sequence.

This is actually somewhat correct behavior (recovering from malformed input),
but we should explicitly handle this case rather than relying on state machine quirks.

## Solution Design

### Option A: Strict ST Validation
Add a dedicated state for "seen ESC in OSC, expecting \":
```rust
enum State { Normal, Esc, Csi, Osc, OscEsc }

State::Osc => {
    if c == '\x07' {
        state = State::Normal;
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        state = State::OscEsc;
    }
}

State::OscEsc => {
    if c == '\\' {
        state = State::Normal;  // Valid ST
    } else if c == '[' {
        state = State::Csi;     // Malformed OSC, start of new CSI
    } else {
        state = State::Esc;     // Other escape sequence
    }
}
```

### Option B: Lenient Recovery (RECOMMENDED)
Keep current behavior but document it explicitly:
```rust
State::Osc => {
    // OSC sequence: ESC ] Ps ; Pt (BEL | ESC \)
    if c == '\x07' {
        // BEL terminator
        state = State::Normal;
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        // Start of ST (ESC \) OR malformed input followed by new escape.
        // Transition to Esc state which will handle either case correctly:
        // - If next char is \, Esc state ignores it (simple escape)
        // - If next char is [, Esc state transitions to Csi (new sequence)
        state = State::Esc;
    }
    // All other characters are part of the OSC payload, ignored for width
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn visible_width_osc_termination() {
    // BEL terminator
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x07text"), 4);
    
    // ST terminator (ESC \)
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x1b\\text"), 4);
    
    // Malformed: OSC followed by CSI (no terminator)
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m"), 3);
    
    // Truncated OSC at end
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title"), 0);
    
    // Empty OSC
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]\x07text"), 4);
}
```

## Dependency
This task depends on bd-10h6 (consolidation) because we only need to fix
this in one place after consolidation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OSC termination properly handles both BEL and ST
- [ ] Malformed input recovers gracefully
- [ ] Tests cover all termination scenarios

+{"id":"bd-awkz","title":"lipgloss: Fix incomplete OSC sequence termination handling","description":"# Bug: Incomplete OSC Sequence Termination Handling

## Problem Statement
In lib.rs lines 403-411, the OSC sequence handling doesn't properly validate
the String Terminator (ST) format:

```rust
State::Osc => {
    if c == '\x07' {
        state = State::Normal;  // BEL terminator - correct
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        // Handle ST (ESC \) - we see ESC, transition to Esc to handle the backslash
        state = State::Esc;  // BUG: doesn't verify \ follows
    }
}
```

## Technical Background
OSC (Operating System Command) sequences have format:
```
ESC ] Ps ; Pt BEL      (7-bit, BEL terminator)
ESC ] Ps ; Pt ESC \    (7-bit, ST terminator)
```

The current code sees ESC and transitions to Esc state, but:
1. Doesn't verify the next character is \
2. If the next char is [, it would incorrectly start a new CSI sequence
3. Incomplete ST at end of string leaves state in Esc

## Example Malformed Input
```
\x1b]0;title\x1b[31m  (OSC followed immediately by CSI - no proper terminator)
```
Current behavior: After seeing \x1b in OSC, transitions to Esc, then [ transitions to Csi.
The "0;title" is ignored, and "[31m" is treated as a CSI sequence.

This is actually somewhat correct behavior (recovering from malformed input),
but we should explicitly handle this case rather than relying on state machine quirks.

## Solution Design

### Option A: Strict ST Validation
Add a dedicated state for "seen ESC in OSC, expecting \":
```rust
enum State { Normal, Esc, Csi, Osc, OscEsc }

State::Osc => {
    if c == '\x07' {
        state = State::Normal;
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        state = State::OscEsc;
    }
}

State::OscEsc => {
    if c == '\\' {
        state = State::Normal;  // Valid ST
    } else if c == '[' {
        state = State::Csi;     // Malformed OSC, start of new CSI
    } else {
        state = State::Esc;     // Other escape sequence
    }
}
```

### Option B: Lenient Recovery (RECOMMENDED)
Keep current behavior but document it explicitly:
```rust
State::Osc => {
    // OSC sequence: ESC ] Ps ; Pt (BEL | ESC \)
    if c == '\x07' {
        // BEL terminator
        state = State::Normal;
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        // Start of ST (ESC \) OR malformed input followed by new escape.
        // Transition to Esc state which will handle either case correctly:
        // - If next char is \, Esc state ignores it (simple escape)
        // - If next char is [, Esc state transitions to Csi (new sequence)
        state = State::Esc;
    }
    // All other characters are part of the OSC payload, ignored for width
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn visible_width_osc_termination() {
    // BEL terminator
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x07text"), 4);
    
    // ST terminator (ESC \)
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x1b\\text"), 4);
    
    // Malformed: OSC followed by CSI (no terminator)
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m"), 3);
    
    // Truncated OSC at end
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title"), 0);
    
    // Empty OSC
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]\x07text"), 4);
}
```

## Dependency
This task depends on bd-10h6 (consolidation) because we only need to fix
this in one place after consolidation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OSC termination properly handles both BEL and ST
- [ ] Malformed input recovers gracefully
- [ ] Tests cover all termination scenarios
- [ ] Behavior documented in code comments","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:17:42.853402879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:20:17.318529861Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:20:17.318460262Z","close_reason":"Added OscEsc state to visible_width() for explicit ST validation. Handles ESC+backslash (valid ST), ESC+[ (malformed->CSI recovery), ESC+] (malformed->new OSC), and other malformed cases. Added 13 new tests covering hyperlinks, ST termination, truncated OSC, and malformed sequences. All 226 lipgloss tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ansi-parsing","correctness","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-awkz","depends_on_id":"bd-10h6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:17:42.873853491Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-awkz","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:17:42.869057698Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
